content.full_text:
City Hall
Telephone 330-[illegible]

15900 Main Street
La Puente, California 91744

June 22, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

CAMPAIGN REPORTING EXEMPTIONS PROTESTED

At its meeting of June 14, 1977, the City Council of the City of La Puente
adopted Resolution No. 2248, expressing criticism of a recent FPPC ruling
whereby campaign reporting exemptions were granted to candidates of the
Socialist and Communist Parties. A certified copy of the Resolution is en-
closed for your review.

Sincerely,

GORDON L. OEHL,
City Clerk

cc:    Congressman George Danielson
       Senator Alan Cranston
       Senator S. I. Hayakawa
       State Senator Alfred H. Song
       Assemblyman Joseph Montoya


GLO:rw

RESOLUTION NO. 2248

A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF
LA PUENTE CRITICIZING THE ACTION OF THE FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION IN GRANTING
CAMPAIGN REPORTING EXEMPTIONS TO SOCIALIST
AND COMMUNIST PARTY CANDIDATES

THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF LA PUENTE DOES RESOLVE:

WHEREAS, the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) has recently
granted exemption from campaign reporting requirements to candidates of the Socialist
and Communist parties, while at the same time still requiring accurate and complete
reporting for candidates of any other party; and

WHEREAS, this City Council finds that such discriminatory enforcement
of the regulations pertaining to campaign reporting not only emasculates the letter and
spirit of the Political Reform Act of 1974, but also make a mockery of enforcing the
requirements against members of the Republican and Democratic parties. The benefits
to be derived from campaign reporting are equally necessary or superfluous regardless
of party affiliation.

NOW, THEREFORE, BE AND IT IS HEREBY RESOLVED by the City
Council of the City of La Puente, that the exemption by the Fair Political Practices
Commission above referred to is hereby found by this City Council to be counter-
productive and invidious discrimination in the interpretation of state legislation adopted
by the initiative process.

PASSED AND ADOPTED this 14th day of June, 1977

AYES:     COUNCILMEN: Storing, Palacio, Singer, LeFever, Martin

NOES:     COUNCILMEN: None

ABSENT:   COUNCILMEN: None


                                        /s/ Gerald M. Singer
                                   Mayor of the City of La Puente

ATTEST:
                                   STATE OF CALIFORNIA
                                   COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES: } SS
                                   City of LA PUENTE

/s/ Gordon L. Oehl                 I, [illegible], CITY CLERK OF THE
City Clerk of the City of La Puente    CITY OF LA PUENTE, DO HEREBY CERTIFY THE
                                   FOREGOING TO BE A FULL, TRUE AND CORRECT
                                   COPY OF THE ORIGINAL AS THE SAME APPEARS
                                   ON FILE IN MY OFFICE.
                                   IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I HAVE HEREUNTO SET
                                   MY HAND AND AFFIXED THE SEAL OF THE CITY
                                   OF LA PUENTE, THIS 23rd DAY OF [illegible], 19 77
                                   GORDON L. OEHL, City Clerk
                                   By... [illegible] C. Watson
                                        Deputy
